title: Single-cell total RNA extraction from marine protists (e.g. Acantharia, Strombidium cf basimorphum, and Prymnesium parvum)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ctido","text":"Many marine protists are not culturable and therefore challenging to study, nonetheless, they are essential in all marine ecosystems. The development of single-cell techniques is allowing for more marine protists to be studied. Such genomic approaches aim to help to disentangle heterotrophic processes such as phagotrophy from osmotrophy and phototrophic-induced anabolic activities. This information will then support cellular and metabolic modeling by better elucidating the physiological mechanisms and quantifying their importance in different scenarios.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ni7g","text":"However, single-cell protocols and low input RNA kits for transcriptomics are usually made for and tested with mammalian cells, as such the feasibility and efficiency of single-cell transcriptomics on highly diverse mixotrophic protists are not always known. Often single-cell transcriptomics of microbial eukaryotes shows low transcript recovery rates and large variability.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"575a9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dsgg0","text":"We report on transcriptomic methods that we have successfully performed on single cells of Acantharia, Strombidium cf basimorphum, and Prymnesium parvum.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":103,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":118,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":135,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"lirm","text":"This first protocol outlines the total RNA extraction step utilizing the .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":72,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"6fk5f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"agmk6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":244264,"guid":"4206CB94341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"RNAqueous\u0026trade;-Micro Total RNA Isolation Kit","mol_weight":0,"sku":"AM1931","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/AM1931","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":440,"name":"Thermo Fisher","link":"https://www.thermofisher.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/440.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/440.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"415cr","text":"To avoid possible RNA degradation, try to work quickly in all steps and work   . To help achieve this, do not extract RNA from too many samples simultaneously.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":78,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"12jqh","text":"Always wear clean RNase-free gloves.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3j7p5","text":"Clean workspace with ethanol and an RNase Decontamination Solution.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5qo64","text":"If possible use a dedicated set of pipettes for RNA and use filter tips.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dktu1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"DBA1A2A0323911EBB7C7C54E3A61D174","id":0,"label":"","temperature":"75","unit":165}}}}

steps: Samples need to have been acquired in 100 µL lysis buffer Contributed by users (from the RNAqueous™-Micro Total RNA Isolation Kit Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM1931 ). See our sampling protocol here: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bqvrmw56
Prepare and label two RNase-free tubes per sample, one tube for the total RNA extract and one tube for an aliquot for the Bioanalyzer.

Prepare 12.5 µL, times number of samples of Elution Solution (from RNAqueous™-Micro Total RNA Isolation Kit Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM1931 ) in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes (DNase/RNase free) Contributed by users and put the aliquot in a 75 °C heat block.
For Acantharia samples: thaw and vortex 2 times 00:00:10 all samples (be sure to secure the lids). For other samples types just thaw On ice , and continue with step 6.
Spin down gently
Room temperature for 00:05:00
Add 50 µL 100% Molecular grade ethanol Contributed by users to all samples
Brief vortex and gentle/short spin down
Load the ( 150 µL ) samples each onto a separate Microfilter cartridge (use 100 µL pipet twice)
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 10 seconds (i.e. at maximum)
Add 180 µL of Wash Solution 1 unto the column (Microfilter cartridge)
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at maximum)
Add 180 µL of Wash Solution 2/3 unto the column (Microfilter cartridge)
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at maximum)
Repeat addition of 180 µL of Wash Solution 2/3 unto the column (Microfilter cartridge)
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at max)
Remove and discard all flow-through (pour it out) from the collection/elution tube
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 1 minute (i.e. at maximum) to remove any liquid still on the filter
Replace the collection/elution tube with a clean new one (keeping the top part with filter and RNA!)
Add 6.5 µL Elution Solution (pre-heated at 75 °C ) Do this directly to the center of the filter
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at max)
Add 6.0 µL Elution Solution (pre-heated at 75 °C ) Do this directly to the center of the filter
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00
Centrifuge 10000 x g, °C, 30 seconds (i.e. at max)
Transfer your 12.5 µL RNA from the Elution tube to a (labeled) PCR Tubes & Caps, RNase-free, 0.2 mL (8-strip format) Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM12230 (use 100 µL pipet).
For Bioanalyser take an aliquot of 1.5 µL in a separate (labeled) tube, still On ice . Analyze immediately or store.
Store RNA at -80 °C

Attempt to quantify and assess the quality of total RNA extract using Bioanalyzer. Follow the manufacture's protocol for the Agilent RNA 6000 Pico Kit. Using small single-celled protists RNA is often not measurable.

Total RNA extracted from single cells are often low in concentration and yield. And could in most cases not be measured (e.g. for Strombidium and Prymnesium). For Acantharia (±80 µm diameter) its was measurable but could still be, as low as < 20 pg/µL, hence quantification of RNA(e.g. by Bioanalyzer) can be difficult. For our purposes, we assessed a subset of each biological replicates and if the subset was consistent, we assumed the other replicates were similar. With these single-cell low RNA concentrations, the total RNA extract would anyway be used in subsequent protocols without dilution making accurate quantification of concentrations incidental, and this also minimized cost and workload.

Example of a desirable electrograph showing clear 18S and 28S ribosomal RNA peaks indicating good RNA quality (acantharian sample). The first peak indicates a size and concentration marker of the Bioanalyser chip

Examples of electrographs showing minimal ribosomal RNA peaks. Due to the single-cell approach RNA quantity is generally low (or not measurable) and even in this case, subsequent steps provided quality cDNA product (acantharian sample). The first peak indicates a size and concentration marker of the Bioanalyser chip

Follow up with cDNA synthesis and library preparation for Illumina sequencing cDNA library preparation from total RNA extracts of Single-cell marine protists (e.g. Acantharia, Strombidium basimorphum, and Prymnesium parvum) for transcriptome sequencing by Joost S Mansour
creator: Joost S Mansour
